purpose: Materialize or explicitly simulate a multistate-safe Figure 7 authority-balance export so reviewer-facing surfaces can stop relying on a legacy 21-lineage single-state panel alone.
why_now: The hostile-reviewer sprint prioritizes package readiness, denominator discipline, and exact claim ceilings over more collection.
Shared wording drift for Figure 7 is already mostly closed; the remaining blocker is the missing multistate-safe export or an explicit sidecar-consumption path.

Shard 012 has already hardened figure naming, denominator warnings, and helper-file synchronization, so the next smallest publishability move is an execution-ready request for the unresolved Figure 7 export layer.

current_blocking_evidence.fact: Figure 7 is still a bounded 21-lineage legacy single-state panel; safe reuse still depends on warning-badge logic because the 23-lineage multistate-safe projection is not materialized.
Two singleton-plus-collision rows (LIMEN-000008 and LIMEN-000009) must remain outside the panel until singleton review and identifier-collision protection can travel together.
Shared dashboard-path promotion is blocked on this host and write-enable alone would still not fix the multistate schema/denominator problem.
The exact current-versus-projected Figure 7 family is already validated as 21 legacy versus 23 overlap-aware publication-safe lineages, with the broader duplicate/taxonomy family kept separate at 25.
The main remaining risk is no longer stale wording but failure to carry the full 21/23/25 denominator stack through a true export or explicit sidecar-aware contract.

validated_projection.condition: Only if LIMEN-000008 and LIMEN-000009 travel with singleton-review plus identifier-collision metadata.

broader_non_target_context.meaning: Broader stable-or-standalone duplicate/taxonomy package for the Figure 7 family; not the same denominator as the current panel or the validated projection.

must_produce: One machine-readable multistate-safe Figure 7 export or an explicit sidecar bundle that is mechanically equivalent for reviewer-safe reuse.
One concise schema note explaining exactly which metadata fields prevent the legacy 21-lineage export from being silently overread as the 23-lineage projection.
One patch list showing every shared surface that must move together if the export is consumed.

questions_to_answer: Can the current project produce a reviewer-safe Figure 7 export directly from local artifacts without writing to blocked shared dashboard paths?
If not, what is the smallest explicit sidecar contract that makes the 23-lineage projection reusable without pretending it is already the default materialized panel?
Which exact surfaces must be patched together once the export or sidecar is available so 21, 23, and 25 are not collapsed into one denominator again?
What warning text should remain even after a successful multistate-safe export, especially for multilingual authority caution and broader duplicate/taxonomy context?
allowed_methods: Public/open and local project artifacts only
No authentication, no portal access, no form submission, no publication
Prefer local TSV/Markdown control surfaces over new web retrieval
Treat host-blocked results/dashboard writes as an environment limitation, not a reason to bypass safeguards
Record a negative result if no reviewer-safe export can be materialized under current local constraints
paper_thesis_use: Would unlock a cleaner Figure 7 denominator note for manuscript, dashboard, and thesis surfaces.
Would reduce hostile-reviewer risk that current authority-balance prose is mistaken for the live atlas-wide authority distribution.
Would provide a reusable methods appendix artifact showing how multistate/collision guardrails affect visual denominators.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Figure 7 denominator-stack card: current panel 21, validated projection 23, broader family 25, with explicit warning badges for singleton review and identifier collisions.
success_condition: A future lane either materializes a reviewer-safe boost-local Figure 7 multistate-safe export or leaves a sidecar contract that downstream shared surfaces can cite as mechanically equivalent for 23-lineage reuse.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Use this request as the bounded handoff for the next shard-012 or shard-011 cycle that touches Figure 7, instead of reopening wording-only drift repair or pretending that write-enable alone resolves the denominator problem.
